name: obsidian-default
title: Obsidian Default Experience Pack
description: Default jarvOS profile for making a Markdown vault feel native in Obsidian without making Obsidian a hard foundation.

boundary.principle: Obsidian is the default magical front door for Markdown content; jarvOS remains Markdown-first and adapter-backed.
boundary.taskAuthority: Paperclip remains the live project/status system. Bases and Canvas may summarize work, but never own status.

skills.name: obsidian-markdown
skills.role: Write Obsidian-flavored Markdown with wikilinks, properties, embeds, and callouts.
guardrails: Keep notes portable Markdown first; use Obsidian syntax only when it improves the vault experience.

skills.name: obsidian-cli
skills.role: Interact with a running Obsidian app through the obsidian CLI when available.
guardrails: Detect the CLI before use and fall back to Markdown file operations when unavailable.
Obsidian must be open for CLI-backed operations.

skills.name: defuddle
skills.role: Extract clean Markdown from web pages before turning source material into vault notes.
guardrails: Use for standard web pages, not direct Markdown URLs.

skills.name: json-canvas
skills.role: Create and edit Obsidian Canvas files for maps, diagrams, and visual knowledge layouts.
guardrails: Canvas is an artifact and exploration surface, not the live system of record for tasks or blockers.
Validate JSON and edge references before writing .canvas files.

skills.name: obsidian-bases
skills.role: Create Obsidian Bases views over notes for reading, review, and lightweight knowledge dashboards.
guardrails: Bases are read/review views over notes, not a project-management replacement.
Do not mirror or mutate Paperclip issue status through Bases.

commands.name: obsidian
commands.purpose: Enable obsidian-cli workflows against a running Obsidian app.
commands.installHint: Install or expose the Obsidian CLI, then keep Obsidian open while using CLI-backed commands.
